text.title: JPA/Hibernate: Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Common JPA/Hibernate anti-patterns that produce N+1 queries, data corruption, LazyInitializationException, and schema drift in production.

antipattern.description: spring.jpa.open-in-view=true (Spring Boot default): the EntityManager session is kept open for the full HTTP request lifecycle, including the view/serialization layer.
antipattern.reason: open-in-view allows lazy-loading in controllers and serializers, masking N+1 bugs during development. In production with connection pool pressure, holding sessions through the view layer exhausts the pool. Set open-in-view=false and use explicit fetch strategies.

antipattern.description: CascadeType.ALL on every @OneToMany and @ManyToMany without justification.
antipattern.reason: CascadeType.REMOVE with orphanRemoval=true propagates delete through the owning side. If the child entity is referenced by another entity (e.g., an Order referenced by both User and Invoice), the cascade deletes a row still needed elsewhere.

antipattern.description: equals() and hashCode() implemented on the database-assigned @Id field using Lombok @EqualsAndHashCode(of = "id").
antipattern.reason: Before a new entity is persisted, id is null. Two new entities with null id are considered equal by id-based equals, corrupting Sets and Maps. Use a UUID or business key, or the pattern recommended by Vlad Mihalcea (consistent equals based on type + UUID).

antipattern.description: spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update or ddl-auto=create in production to keep the schema in sync with entity changes.
antipattern.reason: ddl-auto=update cannot drop columns safely, cannot rename columns, and runs Hibernate's schema validation without a rollback mechanism. Schema changes in production require Flyway or Liquibase migrations with review and rollback scripts.

antipattern.description: Changing an @Entity annotation or adding a column without a corresponding Flyway/Liquibase migration file in the same PR.
antipattern.reason: Schema drift: the entity expects a column that does not exist in the database. ddl-auto=validate (the correct production setting) throws on startup. CI passes (uses ddl-auto=create for tests) but production deploy fails.

antipattern.description: Calling findById(id).get() directly instead of findById(id).orElseThrow().
antipattern.reason: Optional.get() throws NoSuchElementException with no context when the entity is missing. orElseThrow(() -> new NotFoundException("User " + id + " not found")) provides a meaningful message and maps to a 404 via the exception handler.

antipattern.description: Accessing lazy collections in a controller method or in a @RestController serializer outside a @Transactional boundary.
antipattern.reason: Hibernate session is closed at the end of the service transaction. Accessing u.getRoles() in the controller triggers a new query on a closed session — LazyInitializationException. Use JOIN FETCH or @EntityGraph inside the service to load required associations before the session closes.
